reasoning: RFC 8446 §9.2 requires TLS-compliant applications to implement all of: supported_versions, cookie, signature_algorithms, signature_algorithms_cert, supported_groups, key_share, and server_name. The function SSL_extension_supported (ssl/statem/extensions_cust.c:621-670) lists internally supported extensions and returns 1 for each one. It includes TLSEXT_TYPE_supported_versions (line 651), TLSEXT_TYPE_cookie (line 654), TLSEXT_TYPE_signature_algorithms (line 637), TLSEXT_TYPE_supported_groups (line 627), TLSEXT_TYPE_key_share (line 628), and TLSEXT_TYPE_server_name (line 635) — but it does NOT include TLSEXT_TYPE_signature_algorithms_cert anywhere in the switch statement. A search of the codebase confirms the type constant exists (used in ssl/statem/extensions.c:338 and ssl/t1_trce.c:498), but it falls through to the 'default: return 0;' branch. Furthermore, a comment at ssl/statem/extensions.c:343 explicitly states 'We do not generate signature_algorithms_cert at present,' confirming this extension is not implemented as a built-in supported extension. This means the function does not declare support for a mandatory extension required by RFC 8446 §9.2.

evidence.expected_behavior: SSL_extension_supported should return 1 for TLSEXT_TYPE_signature_algorithms_cert, as RFC 8446 §9.2 mandates that TLS-compliant implementations support the signature_algorithms_cert extension.
